title: Handle 'COPY --from' in Dockerfile
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Handle the "COPY --from" directives in a Dockerfile.  If we discover this line in our Dockerfiles, the code will create a  new FROM directive for the listed image which will cause the image to be pulled prior to the COPY command is executed.  Prior this was not happening.

Fixes: #1175 

Testing:
```
# cat ~/Dockerfile.copyfrom
FROM php:7.2 
COPY --from=composer:latest /usr/bin/composer /usr/local/bin/composer

# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile.copyfrom -t tom .
STEP 1: FROM composer:latest
```

comments.body: bot doesn't love me, going to close and reopen

Going to need to step back on this one.  
I'm running into issues handling a Dockerfile like:
```
FROM scratch AS myname
COPY test.txt /

FROM scratch
COPY --from=myname /test.txt /etc/test.txt
```
as this change didn't consider that FROM has already been completed for the `COPY --from` construct.  Both instances should work.  With this change I first add a bare 'FROM myname` to the top of the Dockerfile and that fails.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 25d89b4) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Needs a rebase.  Are you still working on this?

Yes, still actively working.  This is one of those dike-plugging boys PR's, I get it working for one type of Dockerfile and then another breaks in the tests.  Mad mess of dependencies between Buildah and imagebuilder.
Well @TomSweeneyRedHat now it needs a rebase.
I hit green buttons over the weekend, made a few changes to make things a bit more expedient and added a couple comments.  Assuming happy tests, should be good for review.

@TomSweeneyRedHat, a meta comment on the commit/PR message. `Addresses` won't close the referenced issue automatically when this PR gets merged. You can pick one of the keywords mentioned here: https://help.github.com/articles/closing-issues-using-keywords/

Personally, I prefer `Fixes: ...` as it aligns (mostly) with the Kernel guidelines.
Thanks for the fixes/addresses tip @vrothberg .  I'll touch the msg up.
@rhatdan yep, set it aside yesterday to look at the memory swap issue and other stuff.  Just jumped back in a bit ago trying to get regexp's to work.  I miss Perl atm.

Per @giuseppe and @vrothberg 's comments, I've converted to use a reexp to parse the strings.  The bonus is that enables me to condense the two loops for ' as|AS ' into one loop.  To make sure we have a test for both instances, I copied the multi-stage-builds test suite to multi-stage-builds-small-as and kept the ' as ' small there and changed ' as ' to ' AS ' in the original.

Tests passed in my sandbox, hopefully the same in the CI system.

@vrothberg I've updated and moved the compiles outside of the for loop, good thought.  I know enough about groups to be dangerous and didn't see a huge buy with them.  However, I did add some TrimSpace() calls to handle something like "COPY --from={tab}myname".  With that in play we now fail in a similar manner as Docker.  

Docker:
```
Step 6/7 : COPY --from=	myname /Dockerfile.name /Dockerfile.name
COPY failed: stat /var/lib/docker/tmp/docker-builder707777720/myname: no such file or directory
```

Buildah
```
STEP 8: COPY --from=	myname /Dockerfile.name /Dockerfile.name
error building at step {Env:[ PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:copy Args:[myname /Dockerfile.name /Dockerfile.name] Flags:[--from=] Attrs:map[] Message:COPY --from= myname /Dockerfile.name /Dockerfile.name Original:COPY --from=	myname /Dockerfile.name /Dockerfile.name}: destination "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/70c29005c032cdce2c216a3646cd7303305c97c64d8acbcbccc9d2d0a87a4b8a/merged/Dockerfile.name" is not a directory
```

Unless you've huge concerns, as this PR fixes the mainline case, I'd like to get this in (assuming happy tests and no mess ups on the new code) and then we can pick at the outliers further if you thinks it's useful/more efficient.

:pushpin: Commit fb9daab has been approved by `vrothberg`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=vrothberg fb9daab167cb00de091a9acbccfc519c02488283 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
